title: Richly Folded Slovakian Sweetened Condensed Milk Pudding
description: A comforting, tangy Slovakian sick-day pudding made by gently folding low-fat condensed milk and sour cream, then baking it slow in a traditional tandoor clay pot.

ingredients.name: egg whites
ingredients.notes: beaten to soft peaks to keep it light

instructions: Preheat your oven or tandoor chamber to 325 degrees Fahrenheit and soak your tandoor clay pot in water for ten minutes.
Whisk the fat free sweetened condensed milk, low fat sour cream, cornstarch, lemon juice, and vanilla extract together in a large bowl until smooth.
Whip the egg whites in a separate clean bowl until they hold soft peaks.
Gently fold the whipped egg whites into the condensed milk mixture in three batches to preserve the airy volume.
Dry the soaked tandoor clay pot and lightly coat the inside with non-stick cooking spray.
Pour the folded pudding mixture into the clay pot and dust the top with a pinch of ground nutmeg.
Cover the clay pot with its lid and bake in the oven for forty minutes until the edges are set but the center still jiggles slightly.
Remove the pot from the heat and let the pudding cool in the clay vessel for fifteen minutes before serving warm.
